description: Add two optional fields to the plugin SDK and all type interfaces throughout the platform: `excludePatterns` (Chrome match patterns for URLs that should NOT match the plugin, solving the Jira/Confluence overlap) and `homepage` (a URL to open when no matching tab exists, enabling future 'open tab' UX). This PRD only adds the fields, validation, and plumbing — no plugins are updated and no UI changes are made.

userStories.title: Add excludePatterns and homepage to shared types and manifest parser
userStories.description: Add optional excludePatterns (string[]) and homepage (string) fields to PluginOpentabsField, PluginManifest, WirePluginPayload, and ConfigStatePlugin in the shared package. Update parsePluginPackageJson to parse and validate both fields. Add unit tests.
acceptanceCriteria: PluginOpentabsField has optional readonly excludePatterns?: string[] and homepage?: string
PluginManifest has optional exclude_patterns?: string[] and homepage?: string (snake_case convention matching url_patterns)
WirePluginPayload has optional excludePatterns?: string[] and homepage?: string
ConfigStatePlugin has optional excludePatterns?: string[] and homepage?: string
parsePluginPackageJson parses opentabs.excludePatterns as an optional string array (validated same as urlPatterns element-wise, but allowed to be absent or empty)
parsePluginPackageJson parses opentabs.homepage as an optional non-empty string
parsePluginPackageJson rejects excludePatterns containing non-string values with a descriptive error
Existing package.json without excludePatterns or homepage still parses successfully (backwards compatible)

New unit tests in manifest.test.ts cover: valid excludePatterns, valid homepage, non-string excludePatterns elements rejected, non-array excludePatterns rejected, non-string homepage rejected, empty string homepage rejected, absent excludePatterns and homepage still parse

userStories.title: Add excludePatterns and homepage to the plugin SDK base class
userStories.description: Add optional excludePatterns and homepage properties to the OpenTabsPlugin abstract base class in the plugin SDK.
acceptanceCriteria: OpenTabsPlugin has optional readonly excludePatterns?: string[] property with JSDoc
OpenTabsPlugin has optional readonly homepage?: string property with JSDoc
Both fields are optional (not abstract) so existing plugins compile without changes
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts (add properties to OpenTabsPlugin class)

Context:
The OpenTabsPlugin class (line 70-147) has abstract readonly fields: name, description, urlPatterns, tools, isReady, displayName. Optional lifecycle hooks follow (teardown, onActivate, etc.).

Changes:
Add two optional (non-abstract) properties after `urlPatterns` (line 79) and before `tools` (line 81):

```typescript
  /**
   * Chrome match patterns for URLs that should NOT match this plugin.
   * Tabs matching both urlPatterns and excludePatterns are excluded.
   * Useful when a broad urlPattern overlaps with another plugin's domain.
   * @see https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/develop/concepts/match-patterns
   */
  readonly excludePatterns?: string[];
  /**
   * URL to open when no matching tab exists and the user triggers an
   * 'open tab' action from the side panel. Should be a concrete URL
   * (e.g., 'https://github.com'), not a match pattern.
   */
  readonly homepage?: string;
```

Constraints:
- These MUST be optional (not abstract). Existing plugins that do not declare these fields must continue to compile.
- Do NOT add these to the re-exports at the top of the file.
- Place them immediately after `urlPatterns` for logical grouping.

userStories.title: Add excludePatterns and homepage validation to plugin-tools build
userStories.description: Update the validatePackageJson and validatePlugin functions in the plugin-tools build command to validate excludePatterns entries through validateUrlPattern and validate homepage as a valid URL.
acceptanceCriteria: validatePackageJson validates each excludePatterns entry through validateUrlPattern (if present)
validatePlugin validates plugin.excludePatterns entries through validateUrlPattern (if present)
validatePlugin validates plugin.homepage is a valid URL if present (using new URL() parse check)
Build succeeds for plugins with no excludePatterns or homepage (backwards compatible)

userStories.title: Plumb excludePatterns and homepage through MCP server loader and state
userStories.description: Add excludePatterns and homepage to LoadedPlugin, RegisteredPlugin, and serializePluginForExtension so the fields flow from plugin discovery through to the extension wire protocol.
acceptanceCriteria: LoadedPlugin interface includes excludePatterns: string[] and homepage: string | undefined
RegisteredPlugin interface includes excludePatterns: string[] and homepage?: string
loadPlugin reads excludePatterns from pkg.opentabs.excludePatterns (defaulting to []) and homepage from pkg.opentabs.homepage
loadPlugin validates each excludePatterns entry through validateUrlPattern
serializePluginForExtension includes excludePatterns and homepage in its return value
ConfigStatePlugin built by buildConfigStatePayload includes excludePatterns and homepage via the serializePluginForExtension spread
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/loader.ts (LoadedPlugin interface, loadPlugin function)
- platform/mcp-server/src/state.ts (RegisteredPlugin interface)
- platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts (serializePluginForExtension function)

Context:
LoadedPlugin (loader.ts:38-62) mirrors the plugin's package.json + manifest data. loadPlugin (loader.ts ~293-427) reads from pkg.opentabs, validates urlPatterns, and returns a LoadedPlugin. RegisteredPlugin (state.ts:93-120) is the server-side runtime representation. serializePluginForExtension (extension-handlers.ts:109-161) builds the WirePluginPayload-like object sent to the extension.

Changes:
1. LoadedPlugin (loader.ts ~38-62): add after `readonly urlPatterns: string[]`:
```typescript
readonly excludePatterns: string[];
readonly homepage: string | undefined;
```

2. loadPlugin — after the existing urlPatterns validation loop (loader.ts ~293-306), add:
```typescript
for (const pattern of pkg.opentabs.excludePatterns ?? []) {
  const patternError = validateUrlPattern(pattern);
  if (patternError) {
    return err(`Invalid exclude pattern in ${dir}: ${patternError}`);
  }
}
```
And in the return value (loader.ts ~409-427), add:
```typescript
excludePatterns: pkg.opentabs.excludePatterns ?? [],
homepage: pkg.opentabs.homepage,
```

3. RegisteredPlugin (state.ts ~93-120): add after `urlPatterns: string[]`:
```typescript
excludePatterns: string[];
homepage?: string;
```

4. serializePluginForExtension (extension-handlers.ts ~109-161): add to the return type annotation and returned object:
```typescript
excludePatterns: plugin.excludePatterns,
homepage: plugin.homepage,
```
Place after `urlPatterns: plugin.urlPatterns`.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change sendSyncFull or sendPluginUpdate — they spread `...serializePluginForExtension()` and will pick up the new fields automatically.
- Do NOT change buildConfigStatePayload — it also spreads `...serializePluginForExtension()` automatically.
- The `validateUrlPattern` import should already exist in loader.ts. Verify before adding a duplicate import.
- RegisteredPlugin is populated from LoadedPlugin in the plugin registry's `register` method — check that the field mapping there includes the new fields. Look for where LoadedPlugin properties are spread/mapped into RegisteredPlugin.

userStories.title: Plumb excludePatterns and homepage through browser extension storage and message router
userStories.description: Add excludePatterns and homepage to PluginMeta, ValidatedPluginPayload, and all the extension code paths that construct these types from wire payloads and build ConfigStatePlugin for the side panel cache.
acceptanceCriteria: PluginMeta includes excludePatterns?: string[] and homepage?: string
ValidatedPluginPayload includes excludePatterns: string[] and homepage?: string
validatePluginPayload extracts excludePatterns (default []) and homepage from the wire payload
toPluginMeta copies excludePatterns and homepage
handleSyncFull cache construction includes excludePatterns and homepage
handlePluginUpdate cache construction includes excludePatterns and homepage
isValidPluginMeta still accepts old stored PluginMeta without excludePatterns (backwards compatible)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/browser-extension/src/extension-messages.ts (PluginMeta interface, line ~219-234)
- platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts (ValidatedPluginPayload ~120-138, validatePluginPayload ~162-236, toPluginMeta ~140-155, handleSyncFull cache ~352-377, handlePluginUpdate cache ~458-472)

Context:
PluginMeta (extension-messages.ts:219-234) stores plugin data in chrome.storage.local. ValidatedPluginPayload (message-router.ts:120-138) is the validated version of the wire payload. validatePluginPayload (message-router.ts:162-236) extracts and validates fields from the raw wire JSON. toPluginMeta (message-router.ts:140-155) maps ValidatedPluginPayload to PluginMeta.

Changes:
1. PluginMeta (extension-messages.ts ~219-234): add after `urlPatterns: string[]`:
```typescript
excludePatterns?: string[];
homepage?: string;
```

2. ValidatedPluginPayload (message-router.ts ~120-138): add after `urlPatterns: string[]`:
```typescript
excludePatterns: string[];
homepage?: string;
```

3. validatePluginPayload (message-router.ts ~184-186): after the urlPatterns extraction:
```typescript
const excludePatterns = Array.isArray(obj.excludePatterns)
  ? (obj.excludePatterns as unknown[]).filter((p): p is string => typeof p === 'string')
  : [];
const homepage = typeof obj.homepage === 'string' && obj.homepage.length > 0 ? obj.homepage : undefined;
```
Add both to the returned object:
```typescript
excludePatterns,
homepage,
```

4. toPluginMeta (message-router.ts ~140-155): add:
```typescript
excludePatterns: p.excludePatterns.length > 0 ? p.excludePatterns : undefined,
homepage: p.homepage,
```
(Store excludePatterns as undefined when empty to avoid bloating chrome.storage.local)

5. handleSyncFull cache (message-router.ts ~352-377): add in the map callback after `urlPatterns: p.urlPatterns`:
```typescript
...(p.excludePatterns.length > 0 ? { excludePatterns: p.excludePatterns } : {}),
...(p.homepage ? { homepage: p.homepage } : {}),
```

6. handlePluginUpdate cache (message-router.ts ~458-472): same pattern after `urlPatterns: validated.urlPatterns`:
```typescript
...(validated.excludePatterns.length > 0 ? { excludePatterns: validated.excludePatterns } : {}),
...(validated.homepage ? { homepage: validated.homepage } : {}),
```

Constraints:
- Do NOT modify isValidPluginMeta (plugin-storage.ts:72-83) — excludePatterns and homepage are optional, so old stored PluginMeta without them will still pass validation.
- Do NOT modify background-message-handlers.ts — the `...metaFields` spread already propagates new PluginMeta fields to the side panel state.
- The `handleSyncFull` and `handlePluginUpdate` cache blocks build ConfigStatePlugin objects — the fields must match the ConfigStatePlugin type updated in US-001.

userStories.title: Add excludePatterns support to tab matching and adapter injection
userStories.description: Update the extension's tab matching logic to respect excludePatterns. Tabs whose URLs match both urlPatterns and excludePatterns should be excluded from plugin matching. Update findAllMatchingTabs, urlMatchesPatterns call sites in injection and dispatch, and add unit tests.
acceptanceCriteria: urlMatchesPatterns accepts an optional second parameter excludePatterns and returns false for URLs matching an exclude pattern
findAllMatchingTabs post-filters tabs to exclude those matching plugin.excludePatterns
injectPluginsIntoTab skips plugins whose excludePatterns match the tab URL
Tab state computation skips tabs that match excludePatterns
Tool dispatch URL validation rejects tabs matching excludePatterns
Unit tests in tab-matching.test.ts cover: URL matching with excludePatterns, URL not matching exclude still matches include, findAllMatchingTabs filters excluded tabs
